title: Sickly Sweet Dutch Fish Stamppot
description: A spicy, comforting Dutch dish featuring cajun-spiced cod, spherified mustard, and a medley of root vegetables, perfect for a sick day.

ingredients.name: Mustard
ingredients.notes: distilled white vinegar and sodium alginate

instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the kale until wilted, about 3 minutes. Reserve.
Meanwhile, spherify the mustard by mixing with sodium alginate and slowly dripping into the boiling water. Cook for 5 minutes, then remove with a slotted spoon and reserve.
In a large skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of butter over medium-high heat. Add the Cajun spice mix and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cod fillets to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes per side, until browned and cooked through. Remove and reserve.
In the same skillet, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter. Add the chopped onions and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the sliced turnips and carrots to the skillet and cook for an additional 5 minutes, until they begin to brown.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the reserved kale and spherified mustard to the skillet and stir to combine.
Return the cooked cod fillets to the skillet and stir to combine with the vegetables and mustard.
Serve the stamppot hot, topped with additional Cajun spice mix and brown sauce if desired.
